question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=gt:0` without any `serialnumber` filter to cause `getLowerFilters` to place only the primary lower bound in the lower part and produce no inner or upper parts, returning NFT history sorted only by token ID without serial number ordering, reorganizing the presentation of multi-serial NFT history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validateFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `spender.id=lt:X&spender.id=lte:Y` to trigger the `validateFilters` rejection (more than one lt/lte), and then use the resulting error response to probe the valid range of spender IDs, using that information to craft a subsequent request that reorganizes NFT history to a specific spender range?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `serialnumber=gt:Y` without any `token.id` filter, which would be rejected by `validateSecondaryBound` in `validateBounds`, and use the error message to enumerate valid filter combinations, then craft a valid request that reorganizes NFT history by serial number range?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `order=asc&timestamp=gte:X` to cause the pagination `next` link to use `lastRow.timestamp` as a `gt:` cursor in ascending order, and then follow that link while switching to `order=desc` to cause the server to interpret the ascending cursor as a descending upper bound, skipping records and reorganizing the reward history across pages?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getPaginationLink() in BaseController] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition `!bounds.next && (isEnd || (primaryBound.hasEqual() && secondaryBound.hasEqual()))` at line 200 of `baseController.js` by supplying both `token.id=eq:X` and `serialnumber=eq:Y` to force `getPaginationLink` to return `null` (no next link), effectively truncating the NFT history to a single record and hiding all other NFT events from paginated consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=gte:X&serialnumber=gte:Y` to cause `getLowerFilters` to produce `[{...primary.lower, operator: eq}, secondary.lower]` — converting the primary lower bound to an equality — and then supply a different `token.id=lte:Z` to cause `getUpperFilters` to return an empty array (since `secondary.hasUpper()` is false), resulting in an asymmetric query that reorganizes the NFT history boundary?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=lte:X&serialnumber=lte:Y` to cause `getUpperFilters` to return `[{...primary.upper, operator: eq}, secondary.upper]`, converting the token ID upper bound to an equality in the upper query part, and thereby reorganize the NFT history so that the last page of results is anchored to a specific token ID rather than a range?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a Hedera account alias (e.g., a public key alias) as `idOrAliasOrEvmAddress` that resolves to a different numeric account ID than expected via `EntityService.getEncodedId`, causing the NFT history of an unintended account to be returned and presenting a reorganized/misattributed NFT transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=eq:X1&timestamp=eq:X2&...&timestamp=eq:Xn` with n equal to `maxRepeatedQueryParameters` to cause `updateQueryFiltersWithInValues` to generate an `IN(...)` clause with n entries, effectively filtering the reward history to only those n timestamps and reorganizing which reward events are visible to the caller?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `limit=1&order=asc` and then follow each `next` link (which uses `lastRow.timestamp` as the cursor) to traverse the entire reward history one record at a time in ascending order, effectively reconstructing a full ascending-order view of the reward history that the default API does not expose?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `spender.id=eq:X1&spender.id=eq:X2&...` (multiple equality spender filters) to populate `spenderIdInFilters` with many entries, causing the NFT query to filter to only those spenders and reorganizing the NFT approval history to show only a crafted subset of approvals?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `spender.id=gte:0` (minimum possible spender ID) to cause `spenderIdFilters` to include a range that covers all spenders, and then combine with `order=asc` to reorganize the NFT approval history from oldest to newest approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=gt:X` with X set to the maximum token ID minus one to cause the multi-union query to return only the last token's NFT history, effectively truncating the visible NFT history to a single token and hiding all other tokens' NFT events?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=gte:X&token.id=lte:Y&serialnumber=gte:A&serialnumber=lte:B` to cause the multi-union query to split into lower, inner, and upper parts, and then exploit the operator transformation in `getLowerFilters` (which converts `primary.lower` to `eq`) to cause the lower part to return records at exactly `token.id=X` with `serialnumber>=A`, reorganizing the boundary records of NFT history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=gte:X&timestamp=lte:X` (same value for both bounds) to cause the SQL query to return only rewards at exactly timestamp X, effectively reorganizing the reward history to a single point in time and hiding all other reward events?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=gte:X` where X is a nanosecond timestamp just after a large batch of rewards was distributed, causing the query to skip all those rewards and return only the most recent ones, reorganizing the visible reward history to exclude a significant historical period?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `limit=N` where N equals the exact number of NFTs owned by an account to cause `getPaginationLink` to return `null` (since `rows.length < limit` would be false only if rows.length equals limit), and then use this to determine the exact NFT count of any account, enabling targeted history reorganization attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=gt:X` without a `serialnumber` filter to cause `getInnerFilters` to return an empty array (since `secondary.hasBound()` is false), collapsing the multi-union query to a single-part query that returns NFT history sorted only by token ID, reorganizing the presentation of NFTs that span multiple serial numbers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `order=asc&limit=100` to cause the pagination `next` link to use `lastRow.timestamp` as a `gt:` cursor, and then follow that link while injecting an additional `timestamp=lt:Z` filter to narrow the window, effectively reorganizing the reward history to show only a specific time slice in ascending order?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=lte:X` where X is a nanosecond timestamp in the middle of a staking period to cause the query to return only rewards before that point, reorganizing the reward history to hide all rewards after that timestamp and misrepresenting the account's total reward history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `serialnumber=gt:0&token.id=gte:0` to cause `getLowerFilters` to produce `[{...primary.lower, operator: eq}, secondary.lower]` — anchoring the lower query part to `token.id=0` — and thereby cause the multi-union query to start from the very beginning of NFT history, reorganizing the traversal to always begin at the earliest token?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `spender.id=gt:0&spender.id=lt:MAX_LONG` to cause `validateFilters` to accept the range (one lt and one gt), and then use this to filter NFT history to only approved NFTs, reorganizing the visible NFT history to exclude unapproved NFTs and misrepresenting the account's full NFT ownership history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `timestamp=eq:X` where X is a timestamp that corresponds to a known staking reward distribution event to cause the query to return only that specific reward, reorganizing the reward history to highlight a single event and hiding all other reward history from the response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getPaginationLink() in BaseController] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `lastRow[primaryBound.viewModelKey]` access at line 213 of `baseController.js` — where `viewModelKey` is `token_id` — by crafting a request where the last NFT in the page has a token ID at a boundary value (e.g., max token ID), causing the `next` link to point beyond the valid range and effectively truncating the NFT history at that page?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=eq:X&serialnumber=eq:Y` to cause `getLowerFilters` to include all filters in the lower part (primary.hasEqual() is true), and `getPaginationLink` to return `null` (both bounds have equal), effectively limiting the NFT history response to a single specific NFT and hiding all other NFT history?
